question: What is the primary cause of hyper-aggregation in a network?
options: The use of multiple paths with equal cost.
The tendency of an IGP to send all traffic over the same links based on lowest cost.
A failure in the MPLS control plane.
The manual configuration of static routes.
correct_answers: The tendency of an IGP to send all traffic over the same links based on lowest cost.

question: How does Traffic Engineering (TE) help alleviate hyper-aggregation?
options: By increasing the bandwidth of saturated links.
By allowing path selection based on criteria other than cost to balance traffic.
By disabling the IGP to prevent automatic routing.
By forcing all traffic into a single high-capacity tunnel.
correct_answers: By allowing path selection based on criteria other than cost to balance traffic.

question: Match the MPLS VPN service to its description.
options: Simulates a point-to-point 'wire' or virtual point-to-point connection.
Acts as a virtual 'switch' with ports across the provider network.
Acts as a virtual router with ports across the provider network.

correct_answers.A: Simulates a point-to-point 'wire' or virtual point-to-point connection.
correct_answers.B: Acts as a virtual 'switch' with ports across the provider network.
correct_answers.C: Acts as a virtual router with ports across the provider network.

question: Match the MPLS label operation to its definition.
options: An MPLS header is inserted into a frame.
An existing MPLS header is exchanged for a new one.
An MPLS header is removed from a frame.

correct_answers.A: An MPLS header is inserted into a frame.
correct_answers.B: An existing MPLS header is exchanged for a new one.
correct_answers.C: An MPLS header is removed from a frame.

question: Which database is created in the control plane by protocols like LDP or RSVP?
options: LFIB (Label Forwarding Information Base)
LIB (Label Information Base)
RIB (Routing Information Base)
FIB (Forwarding Information Base)

question: What is the primary function of the LFIB?
options: Storing reachability information from the IGP.
Storing all labels received from peers in the control plane.
Forwarding frames along a Label Switched Path (LSP) in the data plane.
Generating PATH messages for RSVP.
correct_answers: Forwarding frames along a Label Switched Path (LSP) in the data plane.

question: Match the MPLS header field to its size or description.
options: 20 bits; used for forwarding traffic.
3 bits; used for marking traffic with QoS bits.
1 bit; Bottom of Stack flag.
8 bits; Time To Live measured in MPLS hops.

correct_answers.B: 3 bits; used for marking traffic with QoS bits.
correct_answers.D: 8 bits; Time To Live measured in MPLS hops.

question: In 'pipe mode', what happens to the TTL in a customer packet as it passes through 10 LSR routers?
options: It is decremented by 1 at each hop.
It is decremented by 10 at the first hop.
Nothing happens to the customer TTL.
It is reset to 255 at the ingress.
correct_answers: Nothing happens to the customer TTL.

question: Which set of characteristics defines LDP's label handling?
options: Downstream on Demand, Ordered, Conservative retention
Downstream Unsolicited, Ordered, Liberal retention
Upstream Unsolicited, Independent, Liberal retention
Downstream Unsolicited, Independent, Conservative retention

question: Which protocol uses the 'Downstream On Demand' label distribution method?
options: LDP
OSPF
RSVP
BGP

question: What is an absolute requirement for MPLS to function correctly in a network?
options: A complete and converged IGP.
A dedicated hardware VPN concentrator.
The use of IPv6 for all internal links.
Strict hop-by-hop manual path configuration.

question: Match the LDP type to the tunnel it is typically used for.
options: Used for Transport tunnels.
Used for Service tunnels.

question: Why are 6 LSPs required to implement full connectivity for two clients between two branch offices in the provided scenario?
options: LSPs are bidirectional, so 3 are needed for 2 connections.
LSPs are unidirectional; 2 per client for service (4) and 2 for transport (2).
6 is the default limit for LSPs in any MPLS network.
Each client requires 3 LSPs for primary, secondary, and backup.
correct_answers: LSPs are unidirectional; 2 per client for service (4) and 2 for transport (2).

question: Which Layer 4 protocol is used to establish LDP sessions?
options: UDP
TCP
ICMP
SCTP

question: In an LDP bindings table, what does a label marked with 'N' (Not In Use) signify?
options: The label has been withdrawn by the peer.
The peer is not reachable via any IGP path.
The label was received, but the peer is not the next-hop for that FEC in the IGP.
The router has run out of label space.
correct_answers: The label was received, but the peer is not the next-hop for that FEC in the IGP.

question: In the provided LDP bindings output for R1, why do the first two rows have no egress details?
options: R1 is the eLER for itself, so no further forwarding is required.
The egress interface has failed.
A SWAP operation is pending.
The labels are being withheld by the neighbor.
correct_answers: R1 is the eLER for itself, so no further forwarding is required.

question: Match the RSVP state block to its function.
options: Stores details of a PATH request upon receipt.
Stores label information and matches a corresponding PATH request.

correct_answers.A: Stores details of a PATH request upon receipt.
correct_answers.B: Stores label information and matches a corresponding PATH request.

question: How does RSVP prevent sessions from timing out?
options: By using a keep-alive timer of 1 second.
By periodically repeating the PATH and RESV message sequence.
By monitoring interface status constantly.
By using a secondary transport protocol for heartbeat.
correct_answers: By periodically repeating the PATH and RESV message sequence.

question: Which command is used to verify if an RSVP LSP is operational?
options: ping <destination>
show router mpls lsp
oam lsp-ping <lsp-name>
traceroute <destination>

question: What common configuration error prevents an RSVP LSP from becoming active?
options: Incorrect TTL value.
The path or LSP is in a 'shutdown' state.
Using the wrong IGP protocol.
Too many LSRs in the path.
correct_answers: The path or LSP is in a 'shutdown' state.
